description: Washington Post (Dan Lamothe) reported the Pentagon is preparing for 'weeks of ground operations in Iran,' including possible raids on Kharg Island and coastal sites near the Strait of Hormuz. Special operations and conventional infantry would be involved. White House confirmed preparations but said the president has not made a decision.
source: Washington Post / Al Jazeera / White House
data_point: Ground ops planning — Kharg Island + coastal raids

title: Islamabad four-nation summit convenes — most coordinated diplomatic effort
description: Foreign ministers of Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt convened in Islamabad for a two-day summit — the most coordinated regional diplomatic effort since the war began. All four expressed support for Pakistan hosting direct US-Iran talks. Pakistan FM Dar: both sides 'expressed confidence in Pakistan to facilitate their talks.' Germany's FM expects a direct US-Iran meeting 'very soon.'
source: Dawn / Geo News / AP / Al Jazeera
data_point: Four-nation summit — direct US-Iran talks expected 'very soon'

title: Iran allows 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels through Strait — confidence-building gesture
description: Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels through the Strait of Hormuz (2 per day). Two Pakistani cargo ships were released and escorted. FM Dar addressed announcement to VP Vance and Secretary Rubio — signaling diplomatic intent. Trump reposted on Truth Social. Iran also agreed to humanitarian and agricultural shipments.
data_point: 20 Pakistani ships allowed — 2 per day — diplomatic signal

title: UAE intercepts 16 ballistic missiles and 42 drones in single day
description: On March 29, the UAE intercepted 16 ballistic missiles and 42 drones. Kuwait detected 14 missiles and 12 drones. Saudi Arabia intercepted 10 drones. Iranian attacks on Gulf states continue despite 92% destruction of Navy's largest vessels.
source: UAE MoD / Kuwait MoD / Saudi MoD
data_point: UAE: 16 missiles + 42 drones — Kuwait: 14+12 — Saudi: 10

title: Trump threatens to 'completely obliterate' Iran's infrastructure
description: Trump posted on Truth Social threatening to 'blow up and completely obliterate all of their Electric Generating Plants, Oil Wells and Kharg Island (and possibly all desalinization plants!)' if a deal is not reached and the Strait of Hormuz not reopened. He told the Financial Times his 'preference would be to take the oil' and discussed seizing Kharg Island. Current deadline is April 6.
source: Truth Social / Financial Times / Axios / CNN / Al Jazeera / AP
data_point: April 6 deadline — threatens power plants, oil wells, Kharg, desalination

title: Iran strikes chemical plant, Haifa refinery, Kuwait water plant
description: Iran struck a chemical plant in Ne'ot Hovav (Israel), an oil refinery in Haifa, and a water/electrical plant in Kuwait. Iranian Navy Commander Shahram Irani directly threatened USS Abraham Lincoln, vowing to launch 'various types of sea-to-sea missiles' once the carrier enters firing range.
data_point: 3 infrastructure targets hit — Lincoln directly threatened

title: Brent crude $114.90+ — steepest monthly surge on record
description: Brent crude traded at $114.90-$116+ intraday on Monday, with WTI at $101-$103. Brent's 51% monthly surge is the steepest on record, surpassing the 46% in September 1990 (Gulf War). Dubai physical crude at $126/bbl — gap reflects acute physical supply stress. Goldman Sachs warned that if Hormuz remains at 5% flow for 10 weeks, daily Brent could exceed the 2008 record of $147.
data_point: Brent +51% in March — steepest monthly surge on record

title: Rubio: war to end 'in weeks, not months' — Vance takes lead role
description: Secretary of State Rubio told Al Jazeera that Trump 'prefers diplomacy,' confirmed 'messages and some direct talks' via intermediaries, and stated the Strait 'will reopen one way or another.' VP Vance formally designated lead negotiator. Vance told podcast war will continue 'a little while longer' to 'neuter' Iran. Haaretz: Vance seeking 'a quick exit' with Israeli interests 'not a consideration.'
source: Al Jazeera / Axios / Haaretz / Benny Show podcast
data_point: Rubio: 'weeks, not months' — Vance lead negotiator

title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in Iran attacks
description: Spain closed its airspace to US planes involved in attacks against Iran on March 30, joining a growing list of allies distancing from the US-led campaign.
data_point: Spain closes airspace to US military overflights

title: Iraq PMF convoys enter Iran — Axis of Resistance solidarity
description: Footage on March 29 showed Iraqi Popular Mobilization Forces convoys entering Iran via Khorramshahr, officially as humanitarian aid but signaling Axis of Resistance solidarity. Iran also launched a recruitment campaign called 'Janfada' (Sacrificing Life) for volunteers against potential US ground operations.
data_point: PMF convoys enter Iran — 'Janfada' volunteer recruitment

title: Iran parliament pushes NPT exit — unprecedented nuclear escalation
description: Iran's parliament pushed legislation to exit the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty — unprecedented if enacted. Israeli airstrikes continued targeting nuclear infrastructure. The Wall Street Journal reported Trump was considering a Special Forces operation to seize enriched uranium stored in Iran.
data_point: NPT exit legislation — SF uranium seizure considered

title: Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai
description: The Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi was struck by an Iranian drone at the Port of Dubai on March 31, risking a major oil spill at a critical commercial port. The attack signals Iran's asymmetric capabilities remain potent even as its conventional military is degraded.
data_point: VLCC struck at Dubai port — major oil spill risk

title: Two Chinese container ships transit Hormuz — first cargo since war began
description: On March 30, two Chinese container ships successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz — the first cargo vessels to pass through since the war began. Iran's selective passage system continues, requiring ships to submit cargo/crew details, receive clearance codes, and pay approximately $2 million per crossing in Chinese yuan.
data_point: First cargo transits since war — $2M per crossing in yuan

title: Gas crosses $4.00 nationally — highest since 2022
description: Multiple outlets confirmed the national average gasoline price crossed $4.00/gallon on March 31, the highest since 2022. Up $1.02 in one month from $2.98 pre-war. California leads at $5.88/gallon. Diesel hit $5.42/gallon nationally. The Trump administration is considering waiving the Jones Act to ease distribution.
data_point: $4.00/gal national average — CA $5.88 — diesel $5.42

title: Houthis threaten Bab al-Mandeb closure — dual chokepoint scenario
description: Houthi deputy information minister stated that 'closing the Bab al-Mandeb Strait is among our options.' If executed, this would simultaneously shut down both major global maritime chokepoints — an unprecedented scenario. Cambridge expert Elisabeth Kendall described it as a 'nightmare.' The IRGC has reportedly pushed the Houthis toward a Bab al-Mandeb closure to further strain global markets.
source: Al Jazeera / Reuters / BBC / Elisabeth Kendall
data_point: Dual chokepoint threat — Hormuz + Bab al-Mandeb — 'nightmare' scenario

title: Islamabad peace talks stalled — 30-point gap between proposals
description: The Pakistan-hosted Islamabad diplomatic track (Pakistan-Turkey-Saudi-Egypt) represents the most structured mediation effort yet, but the gap between the US 15-point proposal and Iran's 5 counter-conditions remains vast. White House claims talks are 'going well' while Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf publicly rejected negotiations on March 29. Pakistan confirmed weeks of quiet shuttle diplomacy.
data_point: 15-point US plan vs 5-point Iran counter — vast gap

title: Brent nearing $120 after Al Salmi attack — Brent's +55% monthly record since 1988
description: Brent crude was nearing $120 again on March 31 after the Iranian drone strike on the Al Salmi tanker. Brent's ~55% monthly gain is the largest since inception in 1988. Goldman Sachs warned that if Hormuz flows remain at 5% for 10 weeks, Brent could exceed the 2008 record of $147/bbl. Société Générale projected prices as high as $150/bbl. The IEA called this 'the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.'
source: Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / Société Générale / IEA
data_point: Brent +55% monthly — largest since 1988 — IEA: 'largest disruption in history'

title: WSJ: Trump may end war without securing Hormuz control
description: The Wall Street Journal reported Trump told aides he may end the war without securing Strait of Hormuz control. The White House press secretary confirmed reopening the strait is 'not a core objective.' The April 6 deadline for Iran to reopen the strait is approaching, but this report suggests a possible off-ramp that could reshape war aims.
data_point: Hormuz reopening 'not a core objective' — possible off-ramp

title: 10-year Treasury extreme volatility — swings 12bps intraday
description: The 10-year Treasury yield showed extreme intraday volatility on March 31, swinging 12 basis points between 4.31% and 4.43%. The yield reached 4.48% on March 27 (highest since July 2025). February payrolls showed a loss of 92,000 jobs (healthcare strike + federal downsizing), compounding stagflation fears. Moody's AI recession model registered 49% probability.
data_point: 10Y swings 4.31-4.43% — Moody's recession 49% — 92K jobs lost in Feb

title: GCC joint condemnation — all six Gulf states attacked by Iran for first time
description: For the first time in history, Iran has attacked all six GCC states. On March 26, all six GCC states plus Jordan issued a joint condemnation. UAE hardest hit: 414 ballistic missiles, 1,914 drones, 15 cruise missiles intercepted as of March 29 (11 killed, 178 injured). Kuwait intercepted 97 ballistic missiles and 283 drones. Saudi expelled Iran's military attaché. The 2023 Beijing-brokered Saudi-Iran rapprochement has been 'shattered.'
source: UAE MoD / Saudi FM / GCC Joint Statement / Reuters
data_point: First time all 6 GCC states attacked — Saudi-Iran rapprochement 'shattered'

title: Airstrike hits former US Embassy compound in Tehran
description: An airstrike penetrated the former US Embassy compound on Taleghani Street, controlled by the IRGC since 1979 and home to a Basij-run anti-American museum. Witnesses reported blown-out windows but no visible external impact crater, suggesting an internally detonating penetrator. Russia's embassy confirmed the blast broke doors and windows at St. Nicholas Orthodox Cathedral across the street and partially collapsed an adjacent Russian nursing home.
source: Russian Embassy Tehran / Iranian state media / Reuters
data_point: IRGC-controlled former US Embassy compound struck — Russian facilities damaged

title: Iranian drones strike Kuwait airport fuel depots — massive blaze
description: Iranian drone(s) caused a 'massive blaze' at fuel storage tanks at Kuwait International Airport. Kuwait's Public Authority for Civil Aviation reported 'significant damage' but no injuries. KUNA suggested possible Iraqi militia involvement. The airport has been closed since February 28.
data_point: Kuwait airport fuel depots ablaze — airport closed since Feb 28

title: Three Iranian cruise missiles target Qatar — tanker Aqua 1 hit
description: Three cruise missiles launched from Iran toward Qatar. One struck the QatarEnergy-chartered fuel oil tanker Aqua 1 in northern territorial waters. Two others intercepted. The 21-member crew was evacuated with no casualties or environmental impact. QatarEnergy confirmed the attack.
data_point: Tanker Aqua 1 struck — 21 crew evacuated safely

title: Israeli airstrike on Beirut's Jnah neighborhood kills 7
description: An Israeli airstrike without warning on Beirut's Jnah neighborhood killed 7 people and wounded 26, per the Lebanese Health Ministry's final count. In a separate naval strike on Beirut, Israel killed Hajj Yusuf Ismail Hashem, commander of Hezbollah's Southern Front — described by the IDF as the most senior Hezbollah figure eliminated, with '40+ years of experience.' A second 'senior terrorist' killed in separate Beirut strike.
data_point: 7 killed, 26 wounded — Hezbollah Southern Front commander eliminated

title: IRGC cluster bomb missile strikes Bnei Brak — children critically wounded
description: The first wave of three Iranian ballistic missile salvos carried a cluster bomb warhead that struck Bnei Brak, east of Tel Aviv, spreading submunitions over a residential area. 14-16 people wounded including a 10-12-year-old girl critically injured by glass shrapnel in her bed (Sheba Medical Center: 'teams fighting for her life'), a 13-year-old boy seriously wounded while trying to wake his deaf mother (both unable to reach shelter), and the girl's father in moderate condition. Second and third volleys largely intercepted; a fourth salvo of two missiles toward central Israel also defeated.
source: Sheba Medical Center / IDF / Times of Israel / CNN
data_point: Cluster bomb in Bnei Brak — children critically wounded — most complex Iranian attack of war

title: Trump claims Iran 'asked for CEASEFIRE' — schedules prime-time address
description: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'Iran's New Regime President, much less Radicalized and far more intelligent than his predecessors, has just asked the United States of America for a CEASEFIRE!' Iran has not confirmed any ceasefire request. White House confirmed Trump's first prime-time address to the nation since the war began, scheduled for 9 PM ET — falling on first night of Passover. Content unknown; speculation ranges from withdrawal timeline to escalation to diplomatic breakthrough.
source: Truth Social / White House / Reuters / AP
data_point: Trump claims ceasefire request — 9 PM address — Iran denies

title: Israel strikes Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical factory — 10 nurses killed
description: Israel struck the Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical factory in Tehran, claiming the plant supplied fentanyl precursors for an alleged chemical weapons program. Iran acknowledged the strike destroyed the R&D department, calling it 'a blow to the national medical supply chain.' Ten nurses were killed 'at the scene of their service,' per ISNA.
data_point: Pharmaceutical factory destroyed — 10 nurses killed

title: IRGC threatens 17 American corporate headquarters
description: The IRGC threatened to physically attack 17 American companies — naming Apple, Microsoft, Google, Meta, IBM, HP, Intel, Tesla, Boeing, JP Morgan, and others — if more Iranian leaders are assassinated. Deadline: 8 PM Tehran time (~11:30 AM ET), April 1. The IRGC urged employees to 'immediately distance themselves from their workplaces' and called on residents within 1 km to evacuate.
data_point: 17 US companies threatened — Apple, Microsoft, Google, Meta, Boeing, JPMorgan named

title: Markets surge on peace hopes — S&P best day since May; Brent drops 5%
description: Markets rallied hard on Trump's ceasefire claims and Rubio's 'we can see the finish line' statement. S&P 500 closed March 31 at 6,528.52 (+2.91% — best single-day gain since May). Dow 46,341.51 (+2.49%). Nasdaq 21,590.63 (+3.83%). VIX collapsed 17.5% to 25.25. Asian markets surged overnight: Kospi +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%. Brent dipped to $104.86-105.27, down ~5% from morning levels. Gold rose to $4,746-4,766. Analyst Neil Crosby (Sparta Commodities): 'A ceasefire does not equal supply restoration.'
source: Yahoo Finance / CNBC / Bloomberg / Sparta Commodities

title: Pentagon: 200 dynamic strikes in 24 hours; 11,000+ targets in 30 days
description: At the March 31 Pentagon briefing, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth disclosed 200 'dynamic strikes' in the preceding 24 hours — missions where pilots received new target packages mid-flight from real-time intelligence. General Dan Caine confirmed 11,000+ targets struck in the first 30 days. The IDF separately stated 800+ attack sorties have dropped approximately 16,000 munitions on Iran since February 28. Hegseth noted Iran's drone attacks are 'down 95%' from peak levels.
data_point: 200 dynamic strikes — 11,000+ US targets — 16,000 IDF munitions — drones down 95%

title: Pakistan FM in Beijing — China-Pakistan propose 5-point ceasefire plan
description: Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar traveled to Beijing on March 31 to seek China's role as guarantor for a potential deal — reportedly Iran's precondition for talks. Chinese FM Wang Yi: 'Beijing commends and supports Pakistan's mediation effort.' A China-Pakistan five-point ceasefire plan was proposed. Two possible meeting formats under discussion: (1) Iranian FM Araghchi with US envoys Witkoff/Kushner; (2) VP Vance meeting Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf. The 30-point gap between US and Iranian positions persists.
source: Pakistan FM / Chinese MFA / Axios / Reuters
data_point: China-Pakistan 5-point plan — China as guarantor — 30-point gap persists

title: Gas hits $4.064/gallon — largest monthly jump on record ($1.08)
description: National average gasoline reached $4.064 per gallon (AAA, April 1) — the first time above $4 nationally since August 2022. The monthly jump of $1.08 exceeds the steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and the Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). Mid-grade $4.578, premium $4.943, diesel $5.490. California at approximately $5.89. Year-ago price: $3.201.
data_point: $4.064/gal — $1.08 monthly jump — record — diesel $5.49 — CA $5.89

title: Rubio: 'We can see the finish line' — messages being exchanged with Iran
description: SecState Rubio stated on Fox News: 'We can see the finish line. It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it's coming.' Confirmed 'messages are being exchanged' with Iran and a 'potential for a direct meeting at some point.' Trump in a Telegraph interview said he is 'beyond reconsidering' US NATO membership, calling allies 'cowards.' The April 6 energy infrastructure strike deadline is 5 days away with no confirmed diplomatic framework.
source: Fox News / The Telegraph / Reuters / AP
data_point: Rubio: 'finish line' — messages exchanged — Trump: 'beyond reconsidering' NATO

title: Trump's prime-time address to the nation — 9 PM ET
description: President Trump scheduled his first prime-time address to the nation since the war began for 9 PM ET, April 1 — falling on the first night of Passover. Content unknown. Speculation ranges from announcing a ceasefire framework, to declaring a withdrawal timeline, to escalating with energy infrastructure strikes. The address comes as the April 6 deadline for energy strikes is 5 days away, markets have surged on peace hopes, and Rubio confirmed 'messages are being exchanged' with Iran.
data_point: First prime-time war address — content unknown — April 6 deadline 5 days out

title: Trump delivers prime-time address — no exit strategy
description: ~19-min speech declaring war 'nearing completion' with 2-3 more weeks, threatened to bomb power plants 'simultaneously', said Iran's navy is 'gone', air force 'in ruins', told allies to 'grab' Hormuz, threatened to bomb Iran 'back to Stone Ages'. No ceasefire, troop withdrawal, or ground invasion announced.
data_point: 2-3 more weeks — no exit strategy — threatened power plants — no ceasefire

title: IRGC fires 4 missile salvos at Israel — cluster warheads
description: 4 salvos since midnight with cluster warheads — significant escalation in munition type. 4 civilians wounded in Bnei Brak including 2 infants.
data_point: 4 salvos — cluster warheads — 4 wounded Bnei Brak

title: Hezbollah fires 50+ rockets at northern Israel
description: Hezbollah launched barrages of 50+ rockets, 2 lightly wounded. 2026 Lebanon War continues with full IDF ground ops. Israel expanding buffer zone to Litani River. 1,200+ killed in Lebanon, 1M+ displaced.
data_point: 50+ rockets — 2 wounded — buffer zone expanding to Litani

title: IDF reports 170 targets struck in prior 24 hours
description: IDF reported hitting 170 targets in prior 24 hours including weapons research and production sites. Century-old medical research center in Tehran struck. Bridge near Tehran, steel plants, pharmaceutical facilities, Bandar Abbas passenger pier, Qeshm Island desalination plant also hit. CNN geolocated large explosions near Baharestan ~13 miles south of Isfahan.
data_point: 170 targets in 24 hrs — medical center — Baharestan explosions

title: Former FM Kharazi seriously injured, wife killed in airstrike
description: Former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi seriously injured, wife killed in airstrike on April 1. Iran described as deliberate attempt to derail diplomacy — Kharazi had been overseeing Pakistan back-channel for possible Vance-Iranian officials meeting. Significant blow to diplomatic track.
source: Iranian state media / Al Jazeera / Axios
data_point: Kharazi injured — wife killed — Pakistan back-channel damaged

title: Iran flatly denies Trump ceasefire claim
description: FM Araghchi told Al Jazeera 'at present there is no negotiation' and trust level 'at zero.' FM spokesman Baqaei called Trump's claim 'false and baseless.' Iran stated it 'will not tolerate this vicious cycle of war, negotiations, ceasefire.' Back-channel discussions through intermediaries ongoing per Axios.
data_point: Iran denies ceasefire — trust 'at zero' — back-channels ongoing

title: Markets reverse after Trump's escalatory speech
description: S&P ~6,522 (-0.81%). Dow futures dropped 670 points overnight (~46,145). Nasdaq ~21,582 (-1.19%). VIX surged to 27.31 (+11.3%). Brent ~$107.15 post-speech surge. WTI ~$105.20 (spikes to $112). Gold ~$4,660 (-3.19% profit-taking). 10Y Treasury 4.38%. Asian selloff: Nikkei -2.1%, Kospi -3.9%, Hang Seng -1%. South Korea urges $17.3B supplementary budget.
data_point: S&P -0.81% — VIX 27.31 — Brent $107 — Asian selloff — Dow futures -670

title: Pakistan acknowledges 'obstacles' in mediation
description: Pakistan acknowledged 'obstacles' at April 2 briefing. China and Pakistan issued joint 5-point peace initiative calling for immediate ceasefire on April 1. Second four-nation ministerial (Pakistan/Saudi/Turkey/Egypt) held in Islamabad March 29-30. 30-point gap persists: US 15-point plan vs Iran 5-point counter. Kharazi airstrike dealt significant blow to diplomatic track.
data_point: Pakistan: 'obstacles' — China-Pakistan 5-point plan — 30-point gap

title: UK-led Hormuz coalition of 35 nations convenes — US absent
description: UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper chaired virtual summit of 35 nations to discuss reopening Strait. US did not participate. PM Starmer: 'will not be easy' — explicitly distanced UK from war. No country willing to force strait while fighting continues. Planned phases: mine clearance once fighting stops, then tanker protection. Macron: using military force 'unrealistic'. Iraq began exporting oil by tanker truck through Syria as alternative.
data_point: 35 nations — US absent — mine clearance first — Macron: 'unrealistic'

title: IRGC names 18 US/allied companies as 'legitimate targets'
description: IRGC issued threat via Sepah News naming 18 companies: Apple, Google, Microsoft, Meta, Nvidia, Intel, IBM, Oracle, Cisco, HP, Dell, Palantir, JPMorgan Chase, Tesla, GE, Boeing, G42 (Abu Dhabi), Spire Solutions (Dubai). Deadline 8 PM Tehran time April 1. Urged evacuation within 1 km. Stated: 'For every assassination, an American company will be destroyed.'
data_point: 18 companies named — Apple, Google, Microsoft, Meta, JPMorgan, Boeing, Tesla

title: UAE intercepts missile near KEZAD — minor damage
description: UAE intercepted missile near Khalifa Economic Zones (KEZAD) on April 2. Minor damage. Since war began, UAE has intercepted 438 ballistic missiles, 19 cruise missiles, 2,012 drones. Previous strikes hit Dubai Fairmont, Kuwait airport, BAPCO, AWS data centers.
data_point: UAE: 438 BMs + 19 CMs + 2,012 drones intercepted since Feb 28

title: War approval collapses to 28% — MTG breaks with Trump
description: YouGov: 28% support war. CNN/SSRS (Apr 1): 34% approve, 66% disapprove. Republican support dropped from 76% to 61% since Mar 2 — 15-point decline. MTG broke sharply: 'All I heard was WAR WAR WAR.' 71% oppose $200B supplemental. Trump overall approval 35-37% (second-term low). Nate Silver net approval -16.7.
data_point: 28% approval — GOP -15 pts — MTG breaks — 71% oppose $200B

title: First LNG tanker attempts Hormuz transit since war began
description: Bloomberg reported first LNG tanker (Omani-flagged Sohar LNG) attempting transit since war began. Windward: only 11 ships transited on March 31. Mar 1-25 transits down 93% to 142. VLCC freight rates at all-time high. Iran's internet blackout enters 20th day — connectivity at 4% (NetBlocks).
data_point: Sohar LNG first LNG transit — 11 ships Mar 31 — internet 4% for 20 days

title: Day 34 summary — April 6 deadline 4 days away
description: Four critical variables in next 96 hours: April 6 power plant deadline, Bush CSG arrival ~Apr 10-12, Pakistan diplomatic track wounded but not dead, domestic approval eroding fast (28-34%). Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct costs. IEA: 400M barrels released. War cost ~$40.6B. Gas $4.02-4.08. S&P ~6,522. Brent ~$107. 15 KIA / 348 WIA.
data_point: ~$40.6B — Apr 6 deadline 4 days — 28% approval — 4 critical variables

title: Markets close — S&P stages dramatic intraday reversal to 6,582.69 (+0.11%)
description: S&P 500 closed at 6,582.69 (+0.11%) after opening with a sharp selloff following Trump's April 1 evening address. Dramatic intraday reversal driven by Iran-Oman Strait of Hormuz transit protocol headlines and short-covering. First weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%). Dow 46,504.67 (-0.13%). Nasdaq 21,879.18 (+0.18%). WTI settled at $111.54 (+$11.42, +11.4%) — first close above $100 since July 2022. Brent $109.03 (+7.8%). WTI-Brent spread inverted. VIX closed at 23.87 (-2.73%).
data_point: S&P 6,582.69 (+0.11%) — WTI $111.54 — Brent $109.03 — first weekly gain

title: Oil majors fall sharply despite surging crude — demand destruction fears
description: XOM dropped ~5.2% to $160.69 and CVX fell ~4.6% to $197.41 despite WTI closing above $111. Paradoxical decline reflects demand-destruction fears at $110+ oil and broader risk-off sentiment. Defense stocks diverged: RTX in $193-197 range, LMT closed near $622.89 (+0.85%, +30% YTD). Reuters analysis noted defense stocks have actually declined entering the conflict's second month. Pentagon announced 7-year framework to triple PAC-3 missile production.
data_point: XOM -5.2% — CVX -4.6% — LMT +30% YTD — PAC-3 tripling

title: Airlines battered — UAL announces 5% capacity cut on jet fuel surge
description: DAL closed near $58.78 and UAL in the $90-95 range, both declining 3%+ on April 2. UAL announced a 5% capacity cut to offset a 60% surge in jet fuel costs since February. Analysts warned 'robust travel demand of 2025 may finally buckle under $5.00-a-gallon gasoline.' Gold futures fell to $4,692.80 (-$120.30, -2.50%) — investors selling gold to raise cash, rotating into surging dollar. 10-Year Treasury at 4.31%. 2-year at 3.79%.
data_point: UAL 5% capacity cut — jet fuel +60% — gold $4,693 — 10Y 4.31%

title: USS Gerald R. Ford departs Split, Croatia — resumes Eastern Med operations
description: CVN-78 Ford departed Split, Croatia on April 3 after completing repairs and crew rest. The Ford suffered a non-combat fire on March 12 in the Red Sea that displaced 600 sailors and damaged 7 berthing compartments. Now on Day ~284 of deployment — approaching record-breaking 11th month. DESRON 2 escorts (DDG-96, DDG-72, DDG-81) transited back through Suez to Mediterranean. Three carrier strike groups now committed to EUCOM/CENTCOM theater.
data_point: Ford resumes ops — Day ~284 — 3 CSGs committed — DESRON 2 in Med

title: Good Friday — U.S. markets closed; gas hits $4.091/gallon nationally
description: U.S. equity, bond, and commodity markets closed for Good Friday. AAA national average reached $4.091/gallon (up from $4.081 on Apr 2), representing a $1.11/gallon surge in 35 days from $2.98 on February 26. California leads at $5.89/gallon; Oklahoma cheapest at $3.27. Diesel averages $5.43/gallon nationally, up 50% year-over-year.

title: Houthis resume Israel attacks but hold fire on Red Sea shipping — Bab al-Mandeb threat escalates
description: After 5-month pause (since Oct 2025 Gaza ceasefire deal), Houthis resumed attacks on Israel on March 28, with subsequent attacks March 30, April 1, and April 2 — all intercepted. Critically, Houthis have NOT resumed attacks on commercial shipping as of April 3. However, military spokesman Brig. Gen. Saree: 'our fingers are on the trigger.' Senior politburo member al-Bukhaiti considering 'naval blockade' targeting 'aggressor countries.' Houthis warned Bab al-Mandeb closure 'likely' if Gulf states join war. ~30 Saudi tankers at Yanbu within Houthi strike range.
data_point: Houthis: attacks on Israel resumed, shipping spared — Bab al-Mandeb closure 'likely'

title: Ceasefire negotiations stalled — Zarif publishes Foreign Affairs compromise proposal
description: Former Iranian FM Javad Zarif published proposal in Foreign Affairs calling for nuclear program limits plus Hormuz reopening in exchange for comprehensive sanctions relief — more moderate than official Iranian stance. VP Vance reportedly communicating with mediators about ceasefire-for-Hormuz deal. Iran specifically requested Vance for direct negotiations, rejecting envoys Witkoff and Kushner. Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey as key mediators. GOP approval dropped from 76% to 61%. Overall support at 28% (YouGov).
data_point: Zarif compromise — Vance lead negotiator — Iran rejects Witkoff/Kushner — 28% approval

title: IAEA severed from Iran — 972 lbs of 60%-enriched uranium unmonitored
description: IAEA communication with Iranian nuclear regulatory authorities severed since ~February 26. Last report (GOV/2026/8, Feb 27) confirmed enrichment to 60% purity. Iran possesses ~972 lbs of 60%-enriched uranium stored in underground tunnel complex at Isfahan — enough for multiple weapons. Fourth undisclosed enrichment facility remains unlocated. Natanz entrance buildings damaged, 'no radiological consequence expected.' No elevated radiation detected in neighboring countries. Iranian officials signaling potential NPT withdrawal. OFAC General License U: ~140M barrels sanctioned Iranian crude authorized through April 19.
data_point: IAEA severed since Feb 26 — 972 lbs 60% uranium — 4th facility unlocated

title: Lebanon war escalates — Netanyahu orders advance to Litani River; 1,318+ killed
description: Hezbollah entered conflict March 2. Israel launched ground operations March 16 with three divisions. Netanyahu ordered expansion toward Litani River March 29. DM Katz declared Israel will occupy southern Lebanon even after war. Israel killed senior Hezbollah commander Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem in Beirut strike April 1. Lebanese casualties: 1,318+ killed (124 children), 3,935+ wounded, 1.2 million displaced (~25% of population). Lebanese government banned Hezbollah military activities, expelled IRGC, cancelled visa-free agreements with Iran.
data_point: 1,318+ killed — 1.2M displaced — Litani advance — Hezbollah banned by govt

title: Day 35 summary — Good Friday pause, April 6 deadline 3 days away
description: Three dynamics for Day 36+: (1) April 6 deadline for Hormuz reopening — Trump threatened power plants and oil infrastructure but showed willingness to extend deadlines; (2) Houthi escalation against commercial shipping — if Bab al-Mandeb closes alongside Hormuz, both chokepoints blocked simultaneously for first time in history; (3) Ford's return and Bush transit give Navy three-carrier coverage by late April at cost of near-zero Pacific carrier presence. War cost ~$41.8B. Gas $4.091. 15 KIA / 348 WIA. 28% approval.
data_point: ~$41.8B — Apr 6 deadline 3 days — 3 CSGs — dual chokepoint risk — 28% approval

title: F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran — first US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003
description: Iran shot down a US F-15E Strike Eagle over Iranian territory — the first confirmed US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since the 2003 Iraq War. One pilot was rescued; the weapons systems officer (WSO) remains missing with search operations underway. During the rescue attempt, an A-10 Warthog was also downed (pilot recovered), and two Black Hawks took small-arms fire. Represents a significant escalation in Iran's air defense effectiveness.
data_point: F-15E shot down — first US aircraft loss since 2003 — pilot rescued, WSO missing — A-10 also downed

title: Pakistan-mediated ceasefire talks collapse — Iran refuses to meet US officials
description: WSJ reported Iran officially informed mediators it is unwilling to meet US officials in Islamabad. Iran also rejected a proposed 48-hour ceasefire per Fars News. Pakistan FM called reports 'baseless' but the trajectory is clear: US demands (zero enrichment, dismantle all nuclear facilities, open Hormuz, end proxy support, full IAEA verification) vs. Iran's five counter-conditions (end all attacks, mechanisms to prevent war resumption, compensation, recognition of Hormuz sovereignty) are irreconcilable. Trump demanded 'unconditional surrender.' Iran called this something to 'take to the grave.'
source: WSJ / Fars News / Pakistan FM / Multiple
data_point: Iran refuses Islamabad meeting — rejects 48-hr ceasefire — positions irreconcilable

title: CMA CGM container vessel first major Western European ship to transit Hormuz
description: A French CMA CGM container vessel (Maltese-flagged) successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz — the first major Western European ship to do so since hostilities began. A Japanese oil tanker followed, hugging the Omani coast. Iran has selectively permitted China- and India-bound vessels to pass with military escort. Pakistan secured a deal for Pakistani-flagged ships.
data_point: First major Western Hormuz transit — CMA CGM Maltese-flagged — Japanese tanker follows

title: SecDef Hegseth forces Army Chief Gen. Randy George into immediate retirement
description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth forced Army Chief of Staff General Randy George into immediate retirement — one of the most consequential Pentagon upheavals in decades. Hegseth has now fired more than a dozen senior military leaders since taking office. The department has been rebranded as the 'Department of War.' The Signal messaging controversy over war planning discussions continues to generate political headaches.
data_point: Gen. George forced out — 12+ senior leaders fired — Pentagon rebranded 'Department of War'

title: IDF reframes Lebanon war goals — disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic' without full occupation
description: On April 3, the IDF reframed its war goals in Lebanon, acknowledging that militarily disarming Hezbollah is 'unrealistic' without occupying all of Lebanon. Lebanon death toll: 1,345+ killed, 3,935 injured, ~1 million displaced (20% of population). 200,000+ have crossed into Syria. The Lebanese government took the unprecedented step of proscribing Hezbollah's military activities. 132,000+ in 620+ collective shelters at 94% capacity. 49 primary healthcare centers and 5 hospitals shut.
data_point: IDF: disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic' — 1,345+ killed — 1M displaced — 200K into Syria

title: FM Araghchi warns Bushehr strikes risk radioactive fallout reaching GCC capitals
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi warned on April 4 that repeated strikes on Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant risk radioactive fallout reaching GCC capitals. Bushehr has been struck four times during Operation Epic Fury. The plant holds 72 tons of fuel and 210 tons of spent fuel. ~480 Russian citizens remain on site. IAEA has warned of potential major radiological incident.
data_point: Bushehr struck 4 times — radioactive fallout risk to GCC — 72 tons fuel on site

title: March jobs report: +178K vs 60K expected — Fed trapped between inflation and growth
description: March jobs report (released Good Friday) showed 178,000 jobs created vs. 60,000 expected — nearly triple forecasts. Unemployment fell to 4.3%. CPI approaching 4%, driven by oil shock and tariff pass-through. The Fed is in an impossible bind: strong jobs plus surging inflation equals zero rate cuts for 2026. New tariffs announced April 2: 50% on steel/aluminum/copper, 100% on patented pharmaceuticals. National debt hit $39 trillion.
data_point: 178K jobs (3x expected) — unemployment 4.3% — CPI ~4% — zero rate cuts for 2026 — $39T debt

title: Trump approval sinks to 35-39.7% — war never commanded majority support
description: Silver Bulletin aggregate (April 3): Trump at 39.7% approve / 57.1% disapprove — first time below 40% in second term. CNN/SSRS (April 1): 35% overall, economy approval career-low 31%, inflation handling 27%. FiftyPlusOne: 37.2% — lowest of any president at this point since FDR. War support: just 38% vs 55% oppose (Silver Bulletin). Pew: 61% disapprove handling; 59% say force was wrong. Even Fox News: 58% opposition. Two-thirds say US should end involvement quickly.
source: Silver Bulletin / CNN / Pew / Fox News / FiftyPlusOne
data_point: 39.7% approve — 61% disapprove handling — 38% support war — never had majority backing

title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages' — April 6 deadline 2 days away
description: On April 2, Trump threatened to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages' and 'completely obliterate' power plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and desalination infrastructure if no deal comes 'shortly.' His April 1 prime-time address stated the operation is 'nearing completion' within 2-3 weeks. The April 6 Hormuz ultimatum deadline is now 2 days away. Iran has shown no willingness to comply.
data_point: Trump: 'Stone Ages' — 'obliterate' infrastructure — Apr 6 deadline 2 days — Iran defiant

title: Day 36 summary — F-15E loss marks inflection as ceasefire dies and deadline looms
description: Day 36 defined by three converging crises: (1) First US combat aircraft loss to enemy fire since 2003 signals Iran adapting its air defenses; (2) Pakistan ceasefire track collapsed with irreconcilable demands; (3) April 6 deadline 2 days away with Trump threatening infrastructure obliteration. Houthis 4 attacks in 7 days with Bab al-Mandeb blockade possible. CMA CGM vessel breakthrough at Hormuz. Markets closed — March jobs +178K. Gas $4.091. ~$43.1B cumulative. Trump approval 35-39.7%. 15 US KIA, WSO missing.
data_point: ~$43.1B — F-15E lost — ceasefire dead — Apr 6 deadline 2 days — Houthis escalating — 35-39.7% approval

title: WSO rescued after 48+ hours evading IRGC inside Iran — CIA deception campaign
description: The F-15E WSO, a Colonel from the 494th Fighter Squadron (RAF Lakenheath), was recovered after evading capture for approximately 48 hours in Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad province. The operation involved dozens of aircraft, a temporary remote airbase established inside Iran, and a CIA deception campaign feeding Iranian forces false intelligence. Two C-130 Hercules became stuck at the improvised airstrip and were intentionally destroyed; three replacement C-130s flown in. Trump confirmed WSO recovered 'safe and sound.'
data_point: WSO rescued — CIA deception op — makeshift airbase inside Iran — 2 C-130s destroyed

title: Iran strikes IDF headquarters vicinity in Tel Aviv and Neot Hovav industrial zone
description: Iran launched ballistic missiles at IDF headquarters vicinity (Kirya compound) in Tel Aviv on April 4 and struck the Neot Hovav industrial zone in southern Israel on April 5 — the third hit on that facility during the war. IDF instructed southern civilians to remain in shelters. Trump posted video of what he called a 'massive strike' on Tehran killing 'many of Iran's military leaders.'
data_point: Iran BMs at IDF HQ Tel Aviv — Neot Hovav 3rd hit — IDF: shelter in place

title: April 6 ultimatum less than 24 hours — Trump: 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day'
description: Trump reaffirmed the April 6 deadline via Truth Social: 'Time is running out — 48 hours before all Hell will reign down on them.' Separately posted: 'Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran.' Sen. Graham confirmed Trump intends to follow through with 'overwhelming military force.' Iran rejected the ultimatum — Gen. Aliabadi called it 'a helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid action' and warned 'the gates of hell will open for you.'
source: Truth Social / AP / Reuters / Sen. Graham
data_point: Apr 6 deadline <24 hours — Trump: 'Power Plant Day' — Graham: 'overwhelming force' — Iran: 'gates of hell'

title: 82nd Airborne deploying 2,000-3,000 paratroopers — Kharg Island seizure discussed
description: Approximately 2,000-3,000 paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division's 1st BCT (Immediate Response Force) deploying to CENTCOM, led by Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier. Combined with Tripoli and Boxer ARGs, approximately 6,000-8,000 US ground troops moving into close proximity to Iran. Primary discussed mission: seizure of Kharg Island, which handles 90% of Iran's oil exports.
source: Reuters / Stars and Stripes / WSJ / Bloomberg
data_point: 82nd Airborne deploying — 6,000-8,000 ground troops — Kharg Island seizure discussed

title: Ceasefire frozen at 2% — Pakistan talks dead, Vance negotiations stalled
description: Pakistan-led mediation 'reached a dead end' — Iran rejected Islamabad meeting. VP Vance conducting indirect talks via Pakistani Field Marshal Asim Munir. Israeli officials suspected of undermining Vance as 'insufficiently hawkish.' UNSC Bahrain resolution delayed to next week. Turkey and Egypt exploring alternative venues. Prediction markets: 2% ceasefire by April 7, 24% by April 30. Former FM Zarif published comprehensive peace proposal calling for enrichment below 3.67%.
data_point: Ceasefire 2% by Apr 7 — Pakistan mediation dead — Vance stalled — Zarif proposal

title: Day 37 summary — WSO rescue demonstrates reach as April 6 deadline approaches
description: Day 37 defined by: (1) Dramatic WSO rescue from inside Iran via CIA-supported deception and improvised airbase — extraordinary operational reach; (2) April 6 ultimatum <24 hours with Trump threatening civilian infrastructure destruction; (3) Ceasefire 2% probability with all diplomatic channels frozen; (4) 82nd Airborne deploying 2,000-3,000 troops with Kharg Island seizure discussed; (5) Moody's recession probability 49% on pre-war data; (6) Trump approval 35%; gas $4.104. ~$44.3B cumulative.
data_point: ~$44.3B — WSO rescued — Apr 6 deadline <24 hrs — ceasefire 2% — recession 49% — 35% approval

title: Trump sets April 7, 8:00 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz
description: President Trump escalates his ultimatum, setting an explicit deadline of Tuesday April 7 at 8:00 PM ET for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatens strikes on power plants and bridges — a deliberate pivot from military to civilian infrastructure targeting. Trump posted a profanity-laden Truth Social ultimatum: 'Open the F***in' Strait, you crazy bastards, or you'll be living in Hell.' Red Cross warns infrastructure targeting could constitute war crimes.
source: White House / Truth Social / Fox News / ICRC
data_point: Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline — threatens power plants and bridges

title: 45-day ceasefire proposal rejected by Iran
description: Egyptian, Pakistani, and Turkish mediators submitted a 45-day ceasefire proposal late Sunday (April 5-6). Iran rejected it, saying it would allow adversaries to regroup. Iran's Foreign Ministry stated negotiations are 'incompatible with ultimatums and threats.' Trump simultaneously told Fox News there's a 'good chance' for a deal — contradictory signaling that has whipsawed markets for weeks.
source: Iran FM / Reuters / AP / Fox News
data_point: 45-day ceasefire rejected — Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey mediation

title: Trump admits arming Iranian Kurdish opposition groups during Fox News interview
description: In a Fox News interview aired April 6, Trump admitted the US had armed Iranian Kurdish opposition groups and protesters during December-January demonstrations — lending credibility to Tehran's longstanding claims that protests were foreign-backed. The admission complicates diplomatic efforts and provides Iran propaganda ammunition.
data_point: Trump confirms US armed Kurdish opposition during Dec-Jan protests

title: OPEC+ agrees to increase output by 206,000 bpd for May — analysts dismiss as 'academic'
description: OPEC+ agreed on April 5 to increase output quotas by 206,000 barrels per day for May. Analysts dismissed the move as 'academic' given that key producers physically cannot export through the blocked Strait of Hormuz, and the increase represents less than 2% of disrupted supply (~12-15M bpd removed from markets).
data_point: +206K bpd — less than 2% of 12-15M bpd disrupted supply

title: Strait of Hormuz: 53 transits last week, up from 36 — still 90%+ below normal
description: Lloyd's List reported 53 transits last week, up from 36 the prior week, but still a fraction of normal volume. The first Western-linked ships transited since war began on April 4 — a French CMA CGM container vessel and a Japanese gas carrier. Over 400 vessels remain anchored in Gulf of Oman. 20,000+ seafarers stranded. QatarEnergy force majeure on LNG since March 4.
data_point: 53 transits/week — still 90%+ below pre-war 130-138/day

title: Markets prepare to reopen after Easter weekend — futures signal volatility
description: US equity and bond markets were closed April 3 (Good Friday) through April 5 (Easter weekend). S&P 500 futures pre-market: ~6,631-6,647. VIX pre-market: 24.48-24.88. Brent crude ~$111.25/bbl. WTI ~$111.81/bbl. Gold ~$4,665-4,677/oz. 10Y yield 4.31%. National gas average $4.119/gal (AAA). Markets must price in WSO rescue, April 7 deadline, ceasefire collapse, and April jobs report (+178K vs 57-65K expected).

title: IAEA: zero access to Iran's enrichment facilities — 440.9 kg at 60%
description: IAEA Director General Grossi confirmed the agency has zero access to Iran's enrichment facilities and cannot verify the current size, composition, or whereabouts of Iran's enriched uranium stockpile. Before the war, Iran possessed 440.9 kg of 60%-enriched uranium — enough for approximately 10 nuclear weapons if further enriched. Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant struck 4 times total, most recently April 4 with impact 75 meters from site perimeter. Rosatom evacuated 198 Russian staff. Grossi: 'I do not believe that Iran's nuclear program can be eliminated solely by military action.'
data_point: 440.9 kg at 60% — enough for ~10 weapons — zero IAEA access

title: Day 38 summary — 48-hour window opens as Trump's Tuesday Hormuz deadline approaches
description: Day 38 defined by: (1) Trump sets Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline threatening power plants and bridges; (2) 45-day ceasefire rejected by Iran; (3) OPEC+ +206K bpd dismissed as 'academic'; (4) Trump admits arming Kurdish groups; (5) IAEA severed — 440.9 kg at 60%; (6) Houthis holding Bab al-Mandeb card; (7) Brent ~$111.25; gas $4.119; (8) S&P futures ~6,631-6,647. ~$45.6B cumulative. War Powers failed both chambers. $200B supplemental faces massive resistance.
data_point: ~$45.6B — Apr 7 deadline — ceasefire rejected — IAEA severed — gas $4.119

title: US strikes 50+ military targets on Kharg Island — Iran's primary oil export hub
description: US conducted overnight strikes on 50+ military targets on Kharg Island, which handles approximately 90% of Iran's oil exports. A US official told NBC the strikes targeted military infrastructure, not oil facilities directly — mirroring the calibrated approach of a March 13 raid. Represents most significant strike on Iran's economic chokepoint since conflict began.
data_point: 50+ targets on Kharg Island — 90% of Iran's oil exports

title: Israel launches wide-scale bridge and railway strikes across Iran — 'Bridge Day' begins early
description: IDF launched a 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting dozens of infrastructure sites' across Iran. Confirmed hits: key bridge near Qom (~80 miles south of Tehran), railway bridge in Kashan (2 killed, 3 injured), Tabriz-Zanjan freeway in northwestern Iran, and railway infrastructure in Karaj. IDF issued 'urgent warning' in Farsi telling all Iranians to stay away from trains and railway lines for 12 hours. Appears to be opening phase of Trump's threatened 'Bridge Day' campaign.
source: IDF / Reuters / Al Jazeera / Iranian state media
data_point: Wide-scale bridge/railway strikes — IDF 12-hour warning to avoid trains

title: 18 civilians including 2 children killed in US-Israeli attack on Alborz Province
description: At least 18 civilians including 2 children killed in US-Israeli attack on Iran's Alborz Province overnight April 6-7. Tehran residential areas also struck with Red Crescent rescue workers responding to collapsed buildings. Six children (4 girls, 2 boys under age 10) killed in overnight strikes on Tehran residential area.
source: Iranian state media / Red Crescent / HRANA
data_point: 18 civilians killed in Alborz — 6 children killed in Tehran overnight

title: IRGC intelligence chief Khademi and Quds Force commander Bakeri killed in Israeli strikes
description: Two high-value IRGC targets killed April 6: Major General Majid Khademi, head of IRGC Intelligence Organization (Iran's top spy chief), and Asghar Bakeri (Yazdan Mir), commander of Quds Force Unit 840 (special operations/covert attacks). Israeli Defense Minister Katz confirmed responsibility. Follow killings of IRGC Navy BG Mostafa Azizi, Artesh Air Defense commander BG Masoud Zare, and IRGC Oil HQ chief Mohammad Reza Ashrafi Ghahi (April 5-6).
source: IDF / Israeli Defense Ministry / Reuters / Iranian state media
data_point: 2 senior IRGC commanders killed — intelligence chief + Quds Force Unit 840 commander

title: Israel strikes Iran's largest petrochemical complex at Asaluyeh and yellowcake facility in Ardakan
description: Israel struck Iran's largest petrochemical complex in Asaluyeh (South Pars), responsible for ~50% of petrochemical production. Separately, a strike hit the yellowcake production facility (Shahid Rezayee Nejad) in Ardakan, Yazd Province. Iran's Atomic Energy Organization condemned the Ardakan strike as a violation of peaceful nuclear facility protections. Three Tehran airports (Bahram, Mehrabad, Azmayesh) also struck targeting Iranian air force aircraft.
source: IDF / IAEA / Iranian Atomic Energy Organization / Reuters
data_point: Asaluyeh (~50% of petrochemical production) + Ardakan yellowcake facility struck

title: Iran uses cluster munitions against Tel Aviv — residential building hit in Haifa killing 4
description: Iran used cluster munitions in a missile attack on the Tel Aviv area April 6, creating approximately 20 impact sites with several injuries. A missile directly hit a residential building in Haifa April 5, killing 4. At least one missile fired toward Israel on morning of April 7. IDF reports approximately 30 Iranian missiles crossed into Israeli territory since April 2, including 3 cluster missiles. Iran also launched 4 cruise missiles at Kuwait, 2 at Qatar, 1 each at UAE and Saudi Arabia — shift toward more cruise missiles.
source: IDF / Haaretz / Al Jazeera / Reuters / CENTCOM
data_point: Cluster munitions in Tel Aviv — 4 killed in Haifa — 30+ missiles since Apr 2

title: IRGC issues most extreme threat: strikes 'outside the region' if civilian infrastructure targeted
description: IRGC's April 7 warning stated that if civilian infrastructure is attacked, Iran will respond 'outside the region' — the most geographically expansive threat to date — and remove all previous restraint against Gulf neighbors. Separately, a coordinated Houthi-Iran-Hezbollah barrage hit Israel with cruise missiles and drones targeting 'vital and military sites.' Houthi deputy information minister confirmed: 'We are in joint coordination with our brothers in Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq.' Camp Buehring in Kuwait reportedly hit by Shahed-136, destroying US Army Chinook.
source: IRGC / Sepah News / Al Masirah / Houthi media / CENTCOM
data_point: IRGC threatens strikes 'outside the region' — coordinated 3-front barrage

title: Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire — submits 10-point counter-proposal demanding permanent war end
description: Iran formally rejected the 45-day ceasefire proposed by Egyptian-Pakistani-Turkish mediators ('Islamabad Accord') on April 6. Iran submitted 10-point counter-proposal demanding permanent end to hostilities, sanctions relief, reconstruction payments, and Strait of Hormuz safe passage protocol. Iranian FM spokesperson: 'A cease-fire means creating a pause to regroup and commit crimes again.' US official called response 'maximalist.' However, mediation channels remain active — Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir in contact 'all night long' with VP Vance, envoy Witkoff, and Iranian FM Araghchi. Direct text messages between Witkoff and Araghchi confirmed by Axios.
source: IRNA / Iranian FM / Axios / Pakistan PM / Reuters
data_point: Ceasefire rejected — 10-point counter-proposal — Witkoff-Araghchi texts confirmed

title: Vance declares 'military goals achieved' — Trump warns 'a whole civilisation will die tonight'
description: Critical divergence in US messaging. VP Vance, speaking in Budapest, stated the US has 'achieved its war goals' and 'this war will be over very soon' — language that could serve as an off-ramp. Meanwhile Trump posted on Truth Social: 'A whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again' and 'Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day.' Netanyahu warned Trump against accepting ceasefire, insisting Iran must surrender all enriched uranium. France warned infrastructure strikes violate international law. UK hosting virtual conference with 41 countries on post-conflict Hormuz security.
source: White House / Truth Social / Reuters / AP / French Presidency / UK FCO
data_point: Vance: 'military goals achieved' vs Trump: 'a whole civilisation will die tonight'

title: WTI spikes toward $115 on Kharg strikes — S&P reverses four-day rally
description: WTI jumped ~2-3% to $112.42-$115.00 on Kharg Island strike reports, with Bloomberg citing prices approaching $115-116/bbl. Brent volatile at $109.87-$111.68. S&P 500 opened ~6,587.66 (down 0.5-0.7% from Apr 6 close of 6,611.83), reversing four consecutive up sessions. VIX rose to 24.88 (+4.2%). Gold range-bound $4,672-4,700/oz. 10Y yield 4.335%. Goldman Sachs warned persistent disruptions could drag S&P to 5,400. Kalshi prediction markets: 60% probability S&P drops below 5,900 in 2026.
source: Bloomberg / CNBC / Yahoo Finance / Goldman Sachs / Kalshi

title: Iran calls for human shields at power plants — WHO reports 307 health facilities damaged
description: Iran's Deputy Sports Minister Alireza Rahimi called on young people, artists, athletes, and students to form 'human chains' around power plants at 2:00 PM local time Tuesday. A Revolutionary Guard general urged parents to send children to man checkpoints. WHO has verified 20+ attacks on healthcare in Iran resulting in at least 9 deaths. Iranian Red Crescent reports 307 health/medical/emergency facilities damaged. UNHCR: up to 3.2 million Iranians temporarily displaced. Iran's internet connectivity at 4% of normal. HRANA: 3,540-3,546 killed (nearly double Health Ministry's 2,076).
source: Iranian state media / WHO / UNHCR / HRANA / IFRC
data_point: Human shields at power plants — 307 health facilities damaged — internet 4%

title: Trump approval on war collapses — only 34% approve military action (CNN)
description: CNN early April poll: only 34% approve military action (down 7 points from war start); 66% disapprove. YouGov: Trump overall approval 35%, Iran war handling 30-33% (down from 39% in early March). Fox News: 59% disapproval — highest in either Trump term. Among independents, approval collapsed from 31% to 22%. GOP Iran approval dropped from 83% to 68%. Only 27% say war worth costs (vs 59% for Iraq in 2003). 71% oppose $200B supplemental. 68% oppose ground troops.
data_point: 34% approve (CNN) — 27% say worth costs — 71% oppose $200B supplemental

title: Day 39 summary — 8 PM ET deadline approaches at conflict's most dangerous inflection point
description: Day 39 defined by: (1) US strikes 50+ targets on Kharg Island; (2) Israel launches wide-scale bridge/railway strikes — 'Bridge Day' begins early; (3) Iran rejects ceasefire, submits 10-point counter-proposal; (4) Vance: 'military goals achieved' vs Trump: 'a whole civilisation will die tonight'; (5) IRGC threatens strikes 'outside the region'; (6) 2 senior IRGC commanders killed; (7) WTI spikes to $115; S&P ~6,588; (8) Iran calls for human shields at power plants; (9) 8 PM ET deadline — binary inflection point between deal and catastrophic escalation. ~$46.9B cumulative.
data_point: ~$46.9B — Kharg strikes — bridge campaign — 8 PM ET deadline — WTI $115

title: Iran drone strike wounds 15 Americans at Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
description: An Iranian drone strike wounded 15 American service members at Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait. Saudi Arabia intercepted 7 ballistic missiles and 18 drones in its Eastern Province. Iran struck Saudi Arabia's Jubail downstream oil facility in retaliation for the Asaluyeh petrochemical strike, with video showing fires. The UAE activated missile defenses. The King Fahd Causeway between Bahrain and Saudi Arabia was closed indefinitely.
source: CENTCOM / Axios / Saudi MoD / Gulf state media
data_point: 15 Americans wounded — 7 BMs + 18 drones intercepted by Saudi — Jubail struck — King Fahd Causeway closed

title: BREAKING: Trump announces two-week ceasefire on Truth Social — Pakistan-brokered deal
description: At 6:32 PM ET — roughly 88 minutes before his own 8 PM deadline to destroy Iran's power grid and bridges — President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire on Truth Social, brokered by Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir. Trump wrote: 'Based on conversations with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir, of Pakistan... subject to the Islamic Republic of Iran agreeing to the COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING of the Strait of Hormuz, I agree to suspend the bombing and attack of Iran for a period of two weeks.' He added: 'We have already met and exceeded all Military objectives' and called Iran's 10-point proposal 'a workable basis on which to negotiate' — a notable softening from his April 6 assessment of 'not good enough.'
source: Truth Social / White House / Reuters / AP / Axios
data_point: Two-week ceasefire — Pakistan brokered — 88 min before 8 PM deadline — 'Islamabad Accords'

title: Iran accepts ceasefire — Araghchi confirms on X — Pakistan declares 'EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY'
description: Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi confirmed acceptance on X shortly after Trump's announcement. Iran's acceptance language was deliberately narrower: 'For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces and with due consideration of technical limitations.' This is coordinated passage, not free transit — Iran retains gatekeeper role. Pakistan's PM Sharif declared it 'EFFECTIVE IMMEDIATELY' and invited both sides to Islamabad for negotiations beginning Friday, April 10. The framework is being called the 'Islamabad Accords.'
source: Iranian FM / X / Pakistan PM / Al Jazeera / Reuters
data_point: Iran accepts — 'coordinated safe passage' not full reopening — Islamabad talks Apr 10

title: Ceasefire takes effect at 8 PM ET — converting Trump's deadline into a start-of-pause
description: The ceasefire took formal effect at 8 PM ET, converting Trump's deadline from an ultimatum into a start-of-pause. VP Vance is expected to lead the US delegation at Islamabad alongside special envoy Steve Witkoff and reportedly Jared Kushner. Iran's delegation will be led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, approved by Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Field Marshal Munir maintained contact with Vance, Witkoff, and Araghchi simultaneously through the night; Witkoff and Araghchi exchanged direct text messages during the final push.
source: White House / Pakistani PM / Reuters / Axios
data_point: Ceasefire at 8 PM ET — Vance leads delegation — Qalibaf for Iran — talks Apr 10

title: Post-ceasefire violations — missiles still flying from Iran — cluster submunitions hit Beersheba
description: Within hours of the 8 PM ET ceasefire, missiles were still being launched from Iran. Cluster submunitions hit Beersheba, lightly injuring three boys. The UAE, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar all issued alerts or activated defenses. Video showed interception of an Iranian missile over Jerusalem. A US defense official told Axios it 'might take time for the ceasefire order to filter down to the lower ranks of the Revolutionary Guards.' Israel retaliated by attacking a radar installation near Tehran.
source: IDF / Axios / CENTCOM / Gulf state media
data_point: Post-ceasefire violations — cluster submunitions in Beersheba — missile over Jerusalem — Israel retaliates on radar

title: UNSC vote: Russia and China veto 11-2-2 Bahrain-led Strait of Hormuz resolution
description: The UN Security Council voted on a Bahrain-led resolution to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Russia and China vetoed it 11-2-2 (Pakistan and Colombia abstaining). Russia and China are proposing a counterresolution calling for a complete cessation of hostilities. The disconnect between diplomatic channels (Pakistan-brokered ceasefire succeeding while UNSC remains paralyzed) underscores the shifting center of gravity in Middle Eastern diplomacy away from traditional Western-led institutions.
data_point: Russia-China veto 11-2-2 — counterresolution proposed

title: Marjorie Taylor Greene calls for 25th Amendment — Tucker Carlson compares Trump to antichrist
description: Trump's earlier 'whole civilization will die tonight' post triggered the most serious bipartisan backlash of his presidency. Marjorie Taylor Greene — a loyal Trump ally — posted '25TH AMENDMENT!!! Not a single bomb has dropped on America. We cannot kill an entire civilization. This is evil and madness.' Tucker Carlson said Trump was threatening 'a war crime, a moral crime' and suggested he might be the antichrist. Alex Jones asked 'how do we 25th Amendment his ass?' Sen. Ron Johnson (R-WI) told the WSJ Trump 'loses me if he attacks civilian targets.' Sen. Lisa Murkowski said the threat 'cannot be excused away.' Approval: Quinnipiac 37%, Reuters/Ipsos 36%.
source: Truth Social / X / WSJ / Fox News / Quinnipiac / Reuters
data_point: Greene: '25th Amendment' — Carlson: 'antichrist' — Quinnipiac 37% — Reuters 36%

title: Oil crashes overnight — Brent down 14-16%, WTI down 15-19% on ceasefire
description: Oil markets staged one of the sharpest single-session drops in history. Brent crude plunged from ~$109.27 to as low as $91.72 (down 14-16%). WTI crashed from ~$115 to ~$95-97 (down 15-19%). But prices remain 40%+ above pre-war levels ($67 WTI, $61 Brent). Dow futures surged 1,000+ points. Gold continued rising to $4,737/oz despite the ceasefire — persistent demand signaling market skepticism that the pause becomes permanent. CNBC noted 'persistent demand for gold and Treasurys pointed to a market still hedging against uncertainty.' 10-year Treasury yields plummeted.
data_point: Brent -14-16% to ~$93 — WTI -15-19% to ~$96 — Dow futures +1,000+ — gold $4,737 still rising

title: Netanyahu: ceasefire does NOT apply to Lebanon — contradicts Pakistan PM
description: Netanyahu explicitly stated the ceasefire does not apply to Lebanon — directly contradicting Pakistan's PM Sharif, who said it covers 'Lebanon and elsewhere.' This is a critical unresolved fault line in the ceasefire framework. Hezbollah had launched 24 attack waves toward northern Israel on April 7 (~40 rockets hitting communities including Nahariya, 2 civilians injured). After the ceasefire announcement, Hezbollah said it halted attacks. Three IDF divisions continue operating south of the Litani River. The Lebanon war has killed 1,422+ with 1 million+ displaced.
source: Israeli PM office / Reuters / Al Jazeera / AP
data_point: Lebanon excluded from ceasefire — Hezbollah halts attacks — IDF continues

title: Pope Leo XIV calls Trump threat 'truly unacceptable' — announces April 11 peace vigil
description: Pope Leo XIV called Trump's threat of civilizational destruction 'truly unacceptable' and announced an April 11 peace vigil at St. Peter's. Spain closed its airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran strikes and withdrew its ambassador from Israel. The ceasefire defused the immediate crisis but the diplomatic fallout from Trump's rhetoric continues. Congress is on two-week recess but Democrats plan to force an Iran war powers vote upon return, led by Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries. The 'No Kings' protest movement is planning its third nationwide march.
data_point: Pope: 'truly unacceptable' — Spain closes airspace — war powers vote planned

title: Ceasefire stabilizes — no large-scale salvos confirmed on April 8
description: After initial post-announcement violations, the ceasefire appeared to stabilize. No large-scale salvos have been confirmed on April 8 beyond the immediate post-announcement window. Both sides appear to be holding. Houthis remained active but calibrated — senior leader Mohammed Ali Al Houthi told CNN they would refrain from attacking Saudi Red Sea ports as long as Riyadh stays out of the war. They threatened to close Bab el-Mandeb but had not done so. No confirmed new Houthi shipping attacks on April 7-8. Trump posted: 'The United States of America will be helping with the traffic buildup in the Strait of Hormuz. Big money will be made.'
data_point: Ceasefire holding — no large-scale salvos Apr 8 — Houthis calibrated — Trump: 'big money'

title: Day 40 summary — ceasefire holds but fault lines threaten Islamabad talks
description: Day 40 defined by: (1) Pakistan-brokered two-week ceasefire announced 6:32 PM ET Apr 7 — 88 min before deadline; (2) Iran accepts within the hour — 'coordinated safe passage' through Hormuz; (3) Oil crashes 14-19% — one of largest single-day drops in history; (4) Dow futures +1,000+ pts — but gold rising signals skepticism; (5) Post-ceasefire violations then stabilize; (6) Netanyahu excludes Lebanon — contradicts Pakistan PM; (7) Negotiating positions remain gulf apart: Iran wants enrichment rights, US wants nuclear dismantlement; (8) Islamabad Accords talks begin Apr 10 — Vance vs Qalibaf; (9) Trump's own party briefly questioned his fitness to command; (10) Two-week clock expires ~Apr 21. ~$47.7B cumulative.
data_point: ~$47.7B — CEASEFIRE — Brent -14% — Islamabad talks Apr 10 — Lebanon excluded — 2 weeks to Apr 21

title: Israel launches 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — 50 fighter jets, 160 munitions, 100+ targets in 10 minutes across Lebanon
description: In the single largest coordinated strike of the entire war, Israel launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' on the morning of April 8. Fifty fighter jets delivered approximately 160 munitions against 100+ targets across Lebanon in 10 minutes — central Beirut, southern Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, Sidon, and Tyre. Strikes hit neighborhoods in central Beirut without prior warning. At least 254 killed and 1,160+ wounded. Lebanon declared April 9 a national day of mourning. UN Human Rights Chief Volker Turk condemned the strikes as 'nothing short of horrific.' Secretary-General Guterres 'strongly condemned' civilian casualties. This is the pivotal event that fractures the 36-hour-old ceasefire's foundations.
source: IDF / Lebanese Health Ministry / UN / Reuters / Al Jazeera
data_point: 254 killed, 1,160+ wounded — single largest coordinated strike of war — 50 jets, 160 munitions, 100+ targets in 10 min

title: Netanyahu press conference: 'The two-week ceasefire does not include Lebanon... We still have some goals to accomplish'
description: Netanyahu stated bluntly at an April 8 press conference that the Lebanon exclusion is deliberate and operational. The White House confirmed this position through Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt. Pakistan's PM Sharif had announced the ceasefire covered 'everywhere, including Lebanon and elsewhere.' Iran's position is that Lebanon is included. European leaders — Germany, France, Italy, UK, Denmark, Spain, Netherlands, Canada, and EU leadership — issued a joint statement demanding Lebanon's inclusion. France's Macron stated Lebanon was 'fully included.' This becomes the core fracture of the ceasefire framework.
source: Israeli PM office / White House / Macron / Reuters / AP
data_point: Lebanon exclusion confirmed — European leaders + EU issue joint statement demanding inclusion

title: IRGC announces re-closing of Strait of Hormuz — accuses Israel of violating ceasefire
description: Iran's response to Eternal Darkness was swift. The IRGC announced it was re-closing the Strait of Hormuz on April 8, accusing Israel of violating the ceasefire. Iran's parliamentary speaker formally accused the US of breaching the agreement. Ship traffic data collapsed: April 7 saw 11 vessels transit via the IRGC-controlled Northern Corridor near Larak Island; April 8 saw only 5 bulk carriers outbound by noon — no major oil tankers from blue-chip operators. MarineTraffic identified just 2 ships by name: Greek-owned NJ Earth and Liberia-flagged Daytona Beach. White House (Leavitt) claimed 'the Strait is open'; Bloomberg, CNBC, and Windward Maritime contradicted — Windward: 'The strait has not reopened — it is in a supervised pause.' Approximately 800 tankers and 3,200 total vessels with ~20,000 seafarers remain stuck inside the Persian Gulf.
source: IRGC / ABC News / Fars News Agency / Bloomberg / CNBC / Windward Maritime
data_point: Hormuz RE-CLOSED by IRGC — 5 bulk carriers outbound Apr 8 noon — no major oil tankers — ~800 tankers stuck

title: CENTCOM April 8 statement: 13,000+ targets struck, 80% of Iran air defenses destroyed, every Iranian submarine sunk
description: Gen. Dan Caine (CJCS) and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth held an April 8 briefing confirming CENTCOM's cumulative damage assessment: 13,000+ targets struck since February 28, including 4,000 dynamic targets. Destroyed ~80% of Iran's air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage facilities, 800 drone storage facilities, 90% of weapons factories, and 150 Iranian warships across 16 classes — every submarine sunk, 97% of naval mines eliminated. Iran's air force has zero operational flights per day (down from 30-100 pre-war). Hegseth and Caine stated US forces will remain in theater. Trump told CNBC April 9 the military is ready for the 'next conquest' and warned forces to remain near Iran.
source: CENTCOM / CJCS Gen. Caine / Secretary Hegseth / CNBC
data_point: 13,000+ targets — 80% air defenses — 150 warships — every submarine sunk — Iran air force 0 flights/day

title: Hezbollah launches retaliatory missiles on northern Israel — ceasefire cycle spirals
description: In the early hours of April 9, Hezbollah launched retaliatory missile attacks on northern Israel in response to Operation Eternal Darkness. Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah called the April 8 strikes 'a grave violation of the ceasefire.' The IDF admitted it overestimated damage to Hezbollah from the 2024 war — the group still has hundreds of launchers and tens of thousands of rockets, rebuilt with new weapons including FPV drones and possibly Russian anti-ship missiles. The escalation cycle on the Lebanon periphery threatens to pull the entire ceasefire down.
data_point: Hezbollah retaliation on northern Israel — 'grave violation of the ceasefire' — IDF admits overestimated 2024 damage

title: Soufan Center: 'Already Tenuous Ceasefire in Iran Hovers on the Verge of Collapse'
description: The Soufan Center's April 9 assessment headline captured the analytical consensus: the Pakistan-brokered ceasefire is functionally collapsing within 48 hours of activation. The ceasefire on direct US-Iran hostilities is technically holding in Iranian airspace as of April 9 — no confirmed US or Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET April 7. But the ceasefire is being torn apart on its periphery by: Israel's Eternal Darkness in Lebanon, Iran's re-closure of Hormuz, and the Houthi/Hezbollah escalation cycle that neither Washington nor Tehran fully controls. Windward Maritime: 'The strait has not reopened — it is in a supervised pause.'
source: Soufan Center / Windward Maritime Intelligence / Bloomberg
data_point: Soufan Center: ceasefire 'hovers on the verge of collapse' — functionally collapsing within 48 hours

title: Markets reverse ceasefire rally — oil rebounds to ~$97-98, VIX climbs toward 26
description: Financial markets gave back significant portions of the April 8 ceasefire rally on April 9. After Brent crashed to $94.75 and WTI to $94.41 on April 8 close (-14 to -16%), both benchmarks rebounded to ~$97-98 on April 9 as Iran's re-closure of Hormuz and ceasefire doubt reasserted the risk premium. Bloomberg April 9 headline: 'Stocks dropped and oil advanced as traders watched to see if the US-Iran ceasefire holds.' S&P 500 declined from the April 8 close of 6,782.81 to ~6,752.64 intraday. VIX rose from 21.04 (-18.39% April 8) back toward 23-26. Dow Jones, Nasdaq, and Russell 2000 all declining. Gold dipped slightly from ~$4,799-4,867 to ~$4,737-4,744. 10Y Treasury yield: ~4.29%. Analysts note a $12-18 'Hormuz risk premium' remains embedded in prices.
data_point: Brent/WTI rebound to ~$97-98 — S&P ~6,752 down from 6,782.81 — VIX rising to 23-26 — $12-18 Hormuz risk premium embedded

title: White House confirms Vance, Witkoff, Kushner to lead US delegation at Islamabad — highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979
description: The White House confirmed on April 8 that VP JD Vance will lead the US delegation to Islamabad for peace talks, accompanied by Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Secretary of State Marco Rubio was also listed in the diplomatic push. This would be the highest-level in-person US-Iran engagement since 1979. Pakistan's PM Sharif initially invited delegations for Friday April 10, but the White House confirmed talks would begin Saturday morning local time (aligning with April 11 Islamabad time). Trump told the New York Post that Vance 'may not attend' due to safety concerns, but the White House subsequently confirmed Vance would lead. Iran specifically preferred Vance as interlocutor due to his pre-war record opposing new conflicts (Al Jazeera).
source: White House / Al Jazeera / New York Post / Reuters
data_point: Vance + Witkoff + Kushner confirmed — highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979 — talks Apr 10-11

title: Bush CSG ETA April 10-12 — dual-carrier capability coincides with Islamabad talks
description: The USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) CSG-10 remains on track to arrive in CENTCOM AOR April 10-12, delivering dual-carrier capability that coincides with the Islamabad Accords talks. USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) remains the primary striking force, operating in the Arabian Sea ~150 miles off Oman's coast per BBC satellite imagery. USS Tripoli (LHA-7) with 31st MEU remains operational. USS Boxer (LHD-4) with 11th MEU departed Pearl Harbor April 1 and was in the western Pacific as of April 8 (Stars & Stripes). 82nd Airborne 1st BCT (~2,000 paratroopers) forward-deployed to undisclosed Middle East locations. Total US forces in theater: ~50,000+ troops, 108 ships (78 USS, 30 USNS per USNI Fleet Tracker April 6). USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) degraded after March 12 fire and repaired in Croatia.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker / BBC / Stars & Stripes / CBS News
data_point: Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12 — Lincoln 150 mi off Oman — 108 ships — ~50,000+ troops — 82nd Airborne 1st BCT forward

title: Pope Leo XIV confirms April 11 Peace Vigil at St. Peter's Basilica
description: Pope Leo XIV — the first US-born pope — has been an active voice for peace throughout the war. On April 7 at Castel Gandolfo, he called Trump's threat against 'the entire people of Iran' as 'truly unacceptable' and urged US citizens to 'work for peace and reject war.' On April 8, he welcomed the ceasefire as 'a sign of living hope.' A Prayer Vigil for Peace is confirmed for Saturday April 11 at St. Peter's Basilica. The USCCB's Archbishop Paul Coakley invited all to join. The vigil timing coincides with the Islamabad Accords talks.
data_point: Apr 11 Peace Vigil at St. Peter's Basilica — coincides with Islamabad talks

title: Day 41 summary — 72-hour window determines ceasefire survival
description: Day 41 defined by: (1) Ceasefire on direct US-Iran hostilities technically holding ~45 hours; (2) Israel's Eternal Darkness in Lebanon April 8 killed 254, wounded 1,160+ — single largest coordinated strike of war; (3) Iran re-closed Hormuz in response, accusing Israel of violating ceasefire; (4) Soufan Center: ceasefire 'hovers on the verge of collapse'; (5) Netanyahu confirms Lebanon exclusion — core fracture; (6) Hezbollah launches retaliatory missiles early Apr 9; (7) Markets reverse — Brent/WTI rebound to ~$97-98, S&P ~6,752 down from 6,782.81 close, VIX climbing toward 26; (8) White House confirms Vance + Witkoff + Kushner for Islamabad talks Apr 10-11; (9) Bush CSG ETA Apr 10-12 — dual-carrier capability; (10) CENTCOM confirms 13,000+ targets, 80% Iran air defenses destroyed, every submarine sunk; (11) Pope Leo XIV Apr 11 peace vigil at St. Peter's; (12) UN Human Rights Chief condemns Lebanon strikes as 'nothing short of horrific.' Next 72 hours deterministic. ~$48.5B cumulative.
data_point: ~$48.5B — ceasefire on verge of collapse — Eternal Darkness kills 254 — Hormuz re-closed — Islamabad talks Apr 10-11 — Pope vigil Apr 11

title: Former Iranian FM Kamal Kharazi dies from US-Israeli airstrike wounds
description: Former Iranian Foreign Minister Kamal Kharazi died April 9 (age 81) from wounds sustained in a US-Israeli airstrike on his Tehran residence on April 1. His wife was killed immediately in the strike. Kharazi had been head of Iran's Strategic Council on Foreign Relations and was involved in Pakistani mediation efforts. He served as Foreign Minister from 1997-2005 under President Khatami.
data_point: Kharazi dies age 81 — head of Strategic Council on Foreign Relations — wife killed in Apr 1 airstrike

title: Israel kills Ali Yusuf Harshi — nephew/secretary of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem
description: Israeli forces struck the Tallet Khayat neighborhood in Beirut on April 9, killing Ali Yusuf Harshi — the nephew and personal secretary of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem. Israel continued strikes in Beirut's southern suburbs throughout the day. Lebanon's cumulative death toll from Israeli operations since March 2 reached 1,888+ killed and 6,092 injured.
data_point: Harshi killed in Tallet Khayat — Qassem's nephew and personal secretary — Lebanon toll: 1,888+ killed

title: Israel conducts 50+ strikes in Lebanon in 24 hours — reduced Beirut targeting under US pressure
description: By April 10, Israel had conducted 50+ additional strikes in Lebanon in 24 hours. Al Jazeera's correspondent noted a marked reduction in Beirut-area targeting, attributed to direct US pressure. Trump told NBC he asked Netanyahu to be 'a little more low-key' in Lebanon to protect the Iran negotiations. IDF has 5 divisions operating in southern Lebanon and has systematically destroyed all main bridges over the Litani River. WHO warned Lebanese hospitals may exhaust vital medical supplies within days. UNICEF reports 600 children killed or injured since the start of Lebanon operations.
source: IDF / Al Jazeera / NBC / WHO / UNICEF / HRW
